_metadata.description: Global Space IXP — a hypothetical constellation of Internet Exchange Point satellites designed to interconnect disparate LEO constellations via inter-satellite links.
sources: Derived from altitude analysis of Starlink, Kuiper, OneWeb, Telesat Lightspeed, IRIS², Guowang and Qianfan constellation parameters.

design_rationale.altitude_selection: 800 km chosen to sit in the natural gap between the dense Starlink/Kuiper cluster (525–630 km) and the upper LEO constellations (880–1325 km). This minimises the maximum vertical separation to any LEO shell while avoiding the crowded altitude bands. Worst-case radial ISL distance: 275 km down to Starlink, 525 km up to Telesat.
design_rationale.inclination_selection: 86.4° near-polar orbit ensures every IXP orbit crosses all other constellation orbital planes regardless of their inclination (33°–98.98°). Matches OneWeb Gen-1 inclination for potential coordination.
design_rationale.walker_star_pattern: Walker Star 48/6/1 — 6 planes with 30° RAAN spacing and a phase offset factor of 1. This yields equatorial ground-track cells of roughly 3 333 km (cross-track) × 5 027 km (along-track), giving a worst-case slant distance of ~3 000 km to the nearest IXP satellite from any point in LEO — well within demonstrated optical ISL range (≤ 5 000 km).
design_rationale.satellite_count: 48 satellites is the minimum Walker Star geometry that keeps worst-case ISL slant distance under ~3 000 km for the chosen altitude. Scaling to 72 (6×12) or 96 (8×12) improves redundancy and reduces handover frequency.

notes: This is a reference/hypothetical constellation for simulation purposes.
All IXP satellites are assumed to carry multi-beam optical ISL terminals capable of simultaneous links to satellites in different constellations.
The 800 km altitude provides ≤ 1.75 ms one-way radial propagation delay to any LEO shell between 525 and 1 325 km.
RAAN spacing of 30° (Walker Star) concentrates coverage in the hemisphere, providing better revisit than a 60° Walker Delta for polar orbits.
Phase offset F=1 staggers satellites between adjacent planes, improving cross-track coverage at the equator.

shells.notes: Single-shell near-polar Walker Star constellation. Crosses all other LEO constellation orbital planes. 30° RAAN spacing, 45° in-plane satellite spacing, 7.5° inter-plane phase offset.

derived_orbital_parameters.description: Pre-computed orbital parameters for the IXP shell

isl_reach.description: Maximum line-of-sight ISL distance and one-way propagation delay from the IXP shell to each target constellation altitude.

coverage_geometry.description: Coverage cell geometry at the equator for the Walker Star 48/6/1 constellation.
coverage_geometry.notes: At higher latitudes the orbit planes converge, reducing cross-track spacing and improving coverage. Polar regions have excellent coverage due to orbital plane convergence.
